experiment: b=7 corrected-onset witness, tetraquadric 3+1 family (tau(7)=20 != rho(7)=21)
role: decisive distinction between the old cubic-crossing threshold tau(b) and the corrected source-target onset rho(b); see Corollary 5.2 and Section 9 of the paper
method: defect = Theorem 5.1 source-target formula; matrix dimensions confirmed by direct construction (build_sparse); dense F_p rank PENDING on an adequate-memory host

(20,20,20).rank_status: PENDING: dense F_p rank of a 54872 x 55566 matrix (~24 GB as flint.nmod_mat) not run in the audit container (3.9 GB). Run scripts/run_b7_decisive.py on a >= ~32 GB host.

(21,21,21).rank_status: PENDING: dense F_p rank of a 64000 x 63888 matrix (~32 GB as flint.nmod_mat) not run in the audit container (3.9 GB). Run scripts/run_b7_decisive.py on a >= ~40 GB host.

interpretation: At d=20 the old tau(7)=20 would call the defect saturated at the ceiling C_7=2688; the source-target law gives 1994, strictly below the ceiling, so saturation is not reached until d=21=rho(7). The predicted values follow from Theorem 5.1, whose two branches were independently reproduced over F_p at b=4 (480) and b=5 (48,158,352,642,960) in this release.
reproduce: python3 scripts/run_b7_decisive.py --check                 # dims only; runs anywhere
python3 scripts/verify_v04.py                              # checks the formula and any present logs
python3 scripts/verify_v04.py --require-b7                 # also fails if b=7 logs absent
status: predicted from proven Theorem 5.1; matrix dimensions verified by construction; dense finite-field rank pending an adequate-memory host
